commit.message:
fix: race condition on track completion — old track re-streamed

Two bugs caused the wrong track to stream after auto-advance:

1. After _signal_track_completion sent update_playing_status(progress=end),
   the Ynison echo triggered false seek detection (drift ~2s), prematurely
   waking get_audio_stream before the actual index change arrived.
   Fix: update _last_progress_ms/time BEFORE sending so echo drift ≈ 0.

2. The premature wake read current_track_id from stale state (old track),
   streamed it at seek=end, then the real track change set seek=248s on
   the NEW track (from the echo of the second update_playing_status).
   Fix: extract _wait_for_track_change() that loops until current_track_id
   actually differs, ignoring same-track echoes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Balance pass: weighted monsters, tavern rest, economy tuning

- Monster spawning now uses dungeon.floor + weighted selection
  (native-floor monsters are most likely; danger scales correctly floor-to-floor)
- Reduced all monster stats slightly for fairer early game
- Skeleton, Zombie, etc. still present but weighted low on floor 1
- Tavern now offers: Rest for 10gp → roll 1d4 HP (gamble)
- Healer gains 'Heal 1 HP (5gp)' button alongside full-heal option
- Starting gold increased 10gp → 25gp (afford a rest or small heal early)
- XP to level halved (300 → 150 per level) so progression feels rewarding
- spawnTreasure fallback now uses dungeon.floor not node depth

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view (#594)

* feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view

- Add ChartPeriodData interface and periods field to ChartDataPayload in types.ts
- Rewrite calculateDailyStats() to collect up to 365 days using consistent pipeline
- Rewrite buildChartData() to compute daily (30 days), weekly (6 weeks), and monthly (12 months) buckets
- Add lastFullDailyStats field to CopilotTokenTracker for full-year data caching
- Add period toggle buttons (Day/Week/Month) to chart webview
- Update summary cards to be period-aware (labels, count, avg)
- Preserve selected period across background chart updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate redundant getSessionFileDetails call in calculateDailyStats

Remove the duplicate statSessionFile + cache lookup that was happening for
every session file in the 365-day window. The SessionFileCache returned by
getSessionFileDataCached() already stores lastInteraction, so calling
getSessionFileDetails() on top was doing double the I/O work per file.

Also pre-warm full-year chart data in background after updateTokenStats()
so the chart panel opens instantly instead of computing 365 days on demand.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: open chart panel immediately; load week/month data in background

Previously the chart tab appeared empty for ~60s because calculateDailyStats()
(365-day scan) was awaited before createWebviewPanel() was even called.

Now the panel opens instantly with the 30-day data already in memory.
Week and Month toggle buttons show a loading indicator (⌛) and are
disabled while the full-year data is computed in the background. Once
ready, an updateChartData message enables them with correct data.

Also adds periodsReady flag to ChartDataPayload / InitialChartData and
CSS for disabled toggle buttons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct period key mismatch causing chart to show black panel

The periods object in buildChartData() used keys 'daily/weekly/monthly'
but getActivePeriodData() looked up data.periods[currentPeriod] where
currentPeriod is 'day'/'week'/'month'. The lookup always returned
undefined, causing a TypeError crash in renderLayout() which was
silently swallowed by void bootstrap(), leaving the root div empty
and the webview panel black.

Fix: rename keys in ChartDataPayload (types.ts), buildChartData()
(extension.ts), and InitialChartData (webview/chart/main.ts) to use
the short form 'day'/'week'/'month' to match ChartPeriod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(session): use retpath=www.yandex.ru for broad domain cookies

The library's refresh_passport_cookies uses retpath=passport.yandex.ru,
so the redirect chain never reaches yandex.ru — cookies only land on
.passport.yandex.ru. Quasar IoT at iot.quasar.yandex.ru needs cookies
on the broader .yandex.ru domain.

Bypass the library and do the auth flow directly with
retpath=https://www.yandex.ru, following the full redirect chain so
cookies are set on .yandex.ru (matching AlexxIT's original approach).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(import_resolver): cover unsafe markdown link and unclosed paren branches

Add two tests that cover previously uncovered branches in
`lint/rules/import_resolver.rs`:

1. `markdown_link_unclosed_paren_ignored` — exercises the else branch of
   `if let Some(paren_close) = after_paren.find(')')` in
   `parse_markdown_links` when `](` appears but has no closing `)`.

2. `path_traversal_in_markdown_link_rejected` — exercises the false branch
   of `if is_path_safe(&link_path)` in `resolve_imports`, confirming that a
   markdown link whose URL contains `../` is not followed.

Before: 93.65% branch coverage (134 missed)
After:  93.74% branch coverage (132 missed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use gh CLI and Bearer auth for setup action latest version fetch (#1921)

* fix: use gh CLI and Bearer auth for setup action latest version fetch

The 'Test Action (Latest Version)' CI job fails with HTTP 403 when
fetching the latest release via the GitHub API. The root cause is
that the action uses 'Authorization: token' header format, which
can be rejected on internal repositories.

Fix:
- Use gh CLI (pre-installed on runners) as primary method for
  resolving the latest release — it handles auth natively
- Fall back to curl with the modern 'Authorization: Bearer' format
  and proper Accept/API-Version headers
- Both approaches use the GITHUB_TOKEN already available in the env

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security, lifecycle and performance hardening (code audit top-5) (#131)

* fix: remediate critical security, performance and lifecycle bugs from code review

- main.py: store background sync task in app.state and cancel it cleanly on shutdown
- database.py: only suppress 'already exists' OperationalError in _migrate_add_columns, re-raise all others
- models.py: push dedup (min-id subquery), search (ilike), and offset/limit into SQL to avoid full-table loads
- models.py: add 5-second timeout on socket.getaddrinfo to avoid blocking DNS resolution
- tenants.py: add explicit _require_admin dependency on all tenant management endpoints
- security.py: replace raw SHA-256 key derivation with HKDF-SHA256 for proper domain separation

refactor: decouple PTY terminal into src/pty-server.ts and src/public/terminal.js

Extract PTY WebSocket handler, ALLOWED_SHELLS, and getDefaultShell from
server.ts into a standalone pty-server.ts module exposing
setupPtyServer(server, verifyWsClient). Extract openPty/closePty from
app.js into terminal.js ES module loaded via <script type="module">.

- src/public/terminal.js: xterm.js lifecycle, WS connection, keyboard shortcut
- server.ts: import setupPtyServer, remove ~65 lines of inline PTY code
- app.js: remove ~100 lines of inline PTY code, fix orphaned btnTerminal ref
- index.html: add terminal.js module script tag
- tests/unit/pty-server.test.ts: 6 tests for extracted exports

* Simplify: remove WorkspaceScope/WorkspaceChannelFlags types, use admin workspace set

* fixed skip test

* Replace Map<Nat,()> with Set<Nat> for adminWorkspaces per reviewer feedback

Use mo:core/Set instead of Map<Nat,()> for the admin workspace membership
set. This is a more semantically appropriate data structure since we only
need set membership operations (contains, add, remove), not key-value
storage.

Updated:
- slack-user-model.mo: SlackUserEntry.adminWorkspaces is now Set.Set<Nat>
- slack-auth-middleware.mo: UserAuthContext.adminWorkspaces is now Set.Set<Nat>
- weekly-reconciliation-runner.mo: Set.contains instead of Map.containsKey
- All Motoko test files and test-canister.mo

commit.message:
feat: align India multilingual platform

Expand language coverage, fix API-AI research contracts, and refresh docs/context for India-first alignment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Workspace-owned prompts: init copies defaults, workspace takes priority

Users of agent-coordinator in other projects can now fully own and
customise their agent prompts:

- agent-coordinator init now copies all default prompts into
  {workspace}/prompts/ so users get editable local copies
- Prompt resolution order flipped: workspace first, package as fallback
  (previously package-first meant local copies were ignored)
- Existing files are never overwritten on init

Result: edit prompts/architect.md in your project, add prompts/my_agent.md,
and the coordinator will use your versions automatically.

Tests: 4 new tests for init prompt copy + 3 updated resolver tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: switch KAPSARC to explore/v2.1 API, Balady to live gateway

- KAPSARC: migrate from dead v2.1 endpoint to working explore/v2.1
  with catalog search + record fetch support
- Balady: replace dead data.gov.sa CKAN proxy with live
  apiservices.balady.gov.sa/v1/momrah-services/open-data
- Both tools verified returning real data
- Updated tests: 121/121 passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add reverse WinDbg prompt bridge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Spec: Architecture spike — tier transaction types (#210) (#212)

Spec: Architecture spike — tier transaction types (#210)

Resolves #210. Discriminated union data model, consultation flow divergence, API shape, gift recipient model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use blog timezone for content lookahead window

GetContentLookAhead was anchored to DateTime.UtcNow, which shifts the
lookahead window relative to the blog owner's local time. For example,
at 8 PM EST (which is already the next day in UTC), the window starts
a day ahead of what the blog owner expects.

Now uses SystemClock.Instance.GetCurrentInstant().InZone(tz) to anchor
the lookahead to the blog's configured timezone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: detect CMS URL changes and reconnect automatically (#87) (#89)

Add a config watch coroutine that polls cms_config.json every 5 seconds.
When the CMS URL changes (e.g. via the Settings page), the client now
closes the WebSocket gracefully and reconnects to the new URL — no
service restart needed.

- Add _config_watch_loop() that runs alongside the message loop
- Clear auth token on URL change (new CMS won't recognise it)
- Remove fragile sudo systemctl restart from the API endpoint
- Add unit tests for config watch detection
- Add integration tests with real WebSocket servers
- Add Playwright E2E tests for Settings page config save
- Bump version to 1.4.1

Fixes #87

Add --speed flag for replay speed control

New CLI option: --speed <multiplier>
  0 = max speed (default for CLI)
  1 = real-time (original PCAP timing)
  5 = 5x accelerated (default for Docker Compose)

Docker Compose defaults to 5x — fast enough to reach RealTime state
quickly while slow enough for interactive WebSocket testing.

Override: REPLAY_SPEED=1 docker compose up

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
